title: Bioinformatics Combined With Biological Experiments to Explore the Promotion of Lung Metastasis by CCL18 in the Immune Microenvironment of Colorectal Cancer.
snippet: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is one of the most prevalent malignant neoplasms. Tumor metastasis represents a significant contributor to unfavorable prognosis, and lung metastasis is the most common extra-abdominal metastasis of CRC. title: Preclinical evaluation of a multi-epitope mRNA vaccine platform for broad and durable SARS-CoV-2 protection.
snippet: The emergence of immune-evasive SARS-CoV-2 variants has exposed limitations in the breadth and durability of protection conferred by current Spike-based vaccines, highlighting the need for next-generation approaches targeting conserved viral regions. Here, we describe the design and preclinical evaluation of an optimized multi-epitope vaccine, CoV2-BMEPu. C...

title: Integrated characterization of distinct immune subtypes linked to drug responsiveness in breast cancer.
snippet: Precision medicine emphasizes accurate patient stratification. However, existing molecular frameworks often fail to fully capture the intrinsic heterogeneity of breast cancer (BRCA). This study aims to establish a novel immune-based classification system using immunological gene sets to elucidate tumor heterogeneity and identify predictive biomarkers for op...

title: A Mitoxyperilysis-Related Single-Cell and Machine-Learning Framework Defines an Immune-Cold Melanoma Phenotype and a Robust Prognostic Signature.
snippet: Mitoxyperilysis is a mitochondria-dependent membrane lysis process driven by innate immune and metabolic cues, yet its clinical relevance in melanoma remains unclear. We analyzed single-cell RNA-seq data (GSE215120) to quantify a mitoxyperilysis-related score (MRS), resolve cell-type heterogeneity, and compare predicted cell-cell communication between MRS-h...

title: Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy in breast cancer: a narrative review.
publicationName: Translational breast cancer research : a journal focusing on translational research in breast cancer
journalName: Translational breast cancer research : a journal focusing on translational research in breast cancer
snippet: Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy (CIPN) remains a major, unresolved complication of breast cancer therapy, often leading to treatment modifications and enduring functional impairment. This review addresses the complex pathogenesis of CIPN, as well as the clinical presentation and impact of CIPN on patient quality of life. The primary objective of....
